subject: Computer Networking
title: DNS: Naming the Internet
description: Learn DNS: Naming the Internet in one minute.

hook.text: Early ARPANET had a single file listing every host and its address. By the 1980s, that file was unmanageable.

buildup.text: Paul Mockapetris invented DNS in 1983 — a distributed system that could scale to millions of names.

discovery.text: DNS uses a tree structure: root at the top, then .com, .org, .edu, then individual domain names below.

twist.text: Before DNS, adding a host meant editing a shared text file and waiting for it to propagate. Manual and slow.

climax.text: DNS made the internet usable. Imagine typing 142.250.80.46 instead of google.com every single time.

punchline.text: DNS gave the internet human-friendly names.

quiz.question: What problem did DNS solve?
options: A single host file couldn't scale to millions of addresses
Packets were arriving out of order
Email couldn't be sent between different networks
